zh.lifeSummary: Margaret Aert van Riel於1500年出生在荷蘭努-布拉班特。她與Barend Pietersz van der Dussen結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1520年之後在她的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Margaret Aert van Riel于1500年出生在荷兰努-布拉班特。她与Barend Pietersz van der Dussen结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1520年之后在她的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Riel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Emile, Normand, Rolande.
German and Dutch: habitational name from any of several places so named in Rhineland and North Brabant.

variants.name: Riehl
meanings: habitational name from a place called Riehl or Rühle.
altered form of the personal name Rühle, a pet form of Rudolf .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: Aert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Gertrude
meanings: From a Germanic female personal name, derived from gār, gēr ‘spear’ + þrūþ ‘strength’. The name is not found in England immediately after the Conquest, but only in the later Middle English period. It may have been introduced by migrants from the Low Countries who came to England in connection with the cloth trade, and was certainly in consistent use in some areas throughout the 16th and 17th centuries, although it was not generally popular until the 19th century, when many medieval names were revived. It has now fallen from favour again.

historicEvents.description: The Netherlands is inherited by the future Habsburg Emperor and King of Spain Charles V.
The Catholic King Charles I of Spain ruled the Netherlands.

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Aert van Riel was born in 1500, in North Brabant, Netherlands. She married Barend Pietersz van der Dussen. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died after 1520, in her hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret Aert van Riel est née en 1500, à Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas. Elle a épousé Barend Pietersz van der Dussen. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée après 1520, dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret Aert van Riel wurde 1500 in Nord-Brabant, Niederlande geboren. Sie hat Barend Pietersz van der Dussen geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb nach 1520 in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret Aert van Riel è nata il nel 1500 nel Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi. Lei sposato Barend Pietersz van der Dussen. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì dopo il 1520, nel sua cittadina.

ja.North Brabant, Netherlands: オランダノオルド-ブラバント
ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Aert van Rielは1500年にオランダノオルド-ブラバントで生まれた。Barend Pietersz van der Dussenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1520年以後に故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Aert van Riel(은)는 1500년 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Barend Pietersz van der Dussen(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1520년 이후 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margaret Aert van Riel nasceu em 1500, em Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos. Ela casou-se com Barend Pietersz van der Dussen. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu após 1520, em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret Aert van Riel родилась в 1500 г. в местности нурд-брабант, Нидерланды. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Barend Pietersz van der Dussen. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла после 1520 г. в её родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Margaret Aert van Riel nació en 1500, en Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos. Se casó con Barend Pietersz van der Dussen. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió después de 1520, en su pueblo.

th.North Brabant, Netherlands: นูรด-แบรบานต, ประเทศเนเธอร์แลนด์
